comments.author: @PlanetVista4K
comments.text:
Discovering Syria — a land where human civilization began 🌍
If you enjoyed the journey, please like, share, and subscribe for more hidden stories. 👉 Where should we explore next?

replies.author: @RemoteEarthJourneys
replies.text: What amazed me most is how Syria preserves so much of humanity's earliest history despite everything it has endured. I'd love to see a documentary about Iraq or Armenia next—both have incredible stories waiting to be told. What country would everyone else choose?

No wonder Angela Mercal allowed many of these people to her country in their darkest hour in history. Thanks for this video.
Btw, this is part of Bactria. The queen of Seleucus I Nicator was from this area. He was the cousin of Alexander the Great. His empire had ladted for four centuries. Perhaps through mitochondrial DNA, my country's royal bloodline (like Kasyapa, creator of Sigiriya, via King Porus or Sinhal) is connected. Thanks again.

comments.author: @TourismeEnSyrieAvecWaddah
comments.text: La Syrie est le berceau de certaines des plus anciennes civilisations de l'histoire. Les visiteurs sont invariablement séduits par le charme du pays et la chaleur de l'hospitalité syrienne, tout en savourant une cuisine délicieuse, des douceurs et la traditionnelle glace orientale. Toute personne souhaitant visiter la Syrie et découvrir sa beauté époustouflante est invitée à me contacter ; je suis guide touristique agréé et spécialiste des sites archéologiques et touristiques du pays. 😊

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Merci beaucoup pour cette information détaillée et cette offre généreuse ! C'est merveilleux de savoir que la Syrie est si riche en histoire et en culture, et que son hospitalité est si chaleureuse.

comments.text:
The authors of this episodes seem uneducated and illiterate. I just watched first few minutes and noted many information being presented as facts. Planet Vista should know about Indian languages like Sanskrit, Tamil and various scripts before claiming that Aramaic being the first language of humans 😂. Looks like the presenter is a gross Churchian and for him Earth was formed 6000 years ago and language/scripts 2000years ago

Also calling Damascus as the oldest city. Please study about Kashi (Varanasi) before making false claims.

Syria was Vedic kingdom before it was destroyed by the Church

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text:
Regarding your comment on the Aramaic language content: We only stated that Aramaic is the mother tongue of Jesus, not that it is the oldest language in the world. However, we believe the video's title was somewhat exaggerated. We apologize for that!

Regarding your comment on the Damascus city content: According to the information we gathered, Damascus is recognized by UNESCO as a city founded in the third millennium BC. As for Varanasi, Hindu legend says that Shiva founded the city 6,000 years ago. However, the official Britannica source states that Varanasi was actually founded in the second millennium BC. Should I believe the legend or the official source?
